DISCHARGE SUMMARY

Patient is a 84-year-old female who presents to the Emergency Department with chief complaint of dizziness and near-syncope. Symptoms began 3 days ago with progressive worsening. Past medical history is significant for Essential (primary) hypertension. Current medications include see medication list. Vital signs on arrival: HR 95, BP 115/100, Temp 103.3F, RR 13, SpO2 88%. Physical examination reveals confused, unable to follow commands consistently. Labs obtained including CBC, BMP, and lactate. Assessment: Essential (primary) hypertension. Plan: oxygen supplementation, nebulizer treatments, and IV steroids. Patient was discharged home with follow-up instructions.

DAILY PROGRESS NOTE - Day 1 of hospitalization

Subjective: Patient reports feeling better today. Pain level 7/10. Sleep interrupted by vital checks. Poor appetite, ate 25% of meals.

Objective:
Vital Signs: HR 68, BP 118/74, Temp 97.5F, RR 22, SpO2 99%
General: Alert, oriented, comfortable
Cardiovascular: Irregular rhythm, no gallops
Respiratory: Scattered wheezes bilaterally
Abdomen: Mild tenderness in RLQ
Extremities: 1+ bilateral lower extremity edema

Assessment/Plan:
1. Acute myocardial infarction: continue current management
2. Mobility: continue current regimen
Continue to monitor. Anticipated discharge in 1-2 days if continues to improve.
